title: Q1 2026 Business Review
audience: Executive leadership team and board observers

slides.headline: Q1 2026 Business Review
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome the leadership team. Set context: this deck covers Q1 financials, three segment highlights, two escalation items, and the H1 hiring plan. Expect 25 minutes for the deck plus 10 for discussion.

content.text: Financial Results and Strategic Actions — April 2026

slides.headline: Agenda
slides.speaker_notes: Walk through the four sections briefly. Flag that slides 6 and 7 are the action items that require board input before the meeting closes.

items: Q1 Revenue and margin performance
Segment highlights: NA, EMEA, APAC
Two operational risks for escalation
H1 2026 hiring plan approval request

slides.headline: Revenue Hit $29.3M, Up 18% YoY
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize that this is the fourth consecutive quarter of accelerating growth. The 4% beat vs. plan is meaningful given the plan was already raised at the start of the year. NA remains the largest segment but APAC is closing the gap.

slides.headline: Gross Margin Expanded 210bps to 64.4%
slides.speaker_notes: The margin story is infrastructure-led. The NA Kubernetes migration completed in February and cut compute costs by 22%. EMEA followed a similar playbook. APAC margin is still negative on an infrastructure basis due to the new Seoul data center, but that normalizes in Q3.

items: NA compute costs fell 22% after Kubernetes migration completed in February
EMEA infrastructure replication of NA playbook delivered 180bps alone
APAC margin drag from Seoul data center normalizes in Q3 2026

source_citations.quote: gross margin of 64.4%, up 210 basis points quarter-over-quarter

slides.headline: APAC Is Now Our Fastest-Growing Segment
slides.speaker_notes: APAC pipeline growth of 31% QoQ is being driven by three new enterprise logos signed in February and March. The Singapore and Tokyo offices are now fully staffed. The risk is that we are pipeline-heavy but conversion lag is still 90+ days, so Q2 recognized revenue from APAC may disappoint before recovering in Q3.

items: Pipeline grew 31% QoQ — second consecutive quarter of segment leadership
Three new enterprise logos signed: two in Singapore, one in Tokyo
90-day retention for APAC cohort up 9 points after onboarding redesign
CAC fell 14% QoQ as referral channel scaled to 28% of new bookings

slides.headline: Two Risks Require Board Attention
slides.speaker_notes: Both risks are flagged as amber. The supplier concentration issue is structural — 71% of our data pipeline volume flows through a single vendor whose contract is up for renewal in August. The EMEA CAC increase is likely cyclical but the data is only two months old so we are not yet confident in the root cause.

items: Risk 1: Supplier concentration — 71% of data pipeline volume through one vendor
Contract renewal due August 2026; no backup vendor currently qualified
Recommended action: begin dual-vendor qualification by May 1
Risk 2: EMEA CAC up 23% over two months — root cause not yet confirmed
Hypothesis: competitive pressure from new entrant in DACH market
Recommended action: EMEA GTM review by end of April

slides.headline: H1 Hiring Plan: 18 Roles, $2.4M Investment
slides.speaker_notes: The 18 roles break down as 9 engineering, 4 data science, and 5 APAC sales. All are backfilled or net-new based on the H2 revenue model. The $2.4M total cost is within the approved opex envelope. We are asking for board ratification today so recruiting can begin in the first week of May.

slides.headline: Q1 Strong — Two Actions Needed Now
slides.speaker_notes: Close with a call to action. The board needs to do two things today: approve the 18-role hiring plan and mandate the supplier diversification review with a May 1 deadline. Everything else is informational.

items: Q1 revenue of $29.3M beat plan by 4% with accelerating YoY growth
Margin expansion and APAC momentum confirm the operating model is working
Action 1: Approve 18-role H1 hiring plan ($2.4M within opex envelope)
Action 2: Mandate supplier diversification review — deadline May 1
